name: trivial_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_1_trivial_alignment(addr: nat, size: nat)
    requires
        size > 0,
    ensures
        aligned(addr, size),
{
}
reason: If every address is aligned to every positive size, the alignment spec is vacuously true and useless

name: alignment_shift_by_one
code:
proof fn phi_2_alignment_shift_by_one(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        s > 1,
    ensures
        aligned(a + 1, s),
{
}
reason: If alignment is preserved when the address is incremented by one, the modular constraint is broken

name: alignment_only_zero
code:
proof fn phi_3_alignment_only_zero(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        s > 1,
    ensures
        a == 0nat,
{
}
reason: If only zero can be aligned to any size greater than one, the spec incorrectly excludes all positive multiples

name: alignment_implies_unit_size
code:
proof fn phi_4_alignment_implies_unit_size(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        a > 0,
    ensures
        s == 1nat,
{
}
reason: If any positive aligned address forces the size to be 1, the spec collapses nontrivial alignments

name: misaligned_offset_accepted
code:
proof fn phi_5_misaligned_offset_accepted(a: nat, s: nat)
    requires
        aligned(a, s),
        s > 1,
    ensures
        aligned((a + s - 1) as nat, s),
{
}
reason: If an address offset by size-1 remains aligned, the spec fails to distinguish aligned from misaligned addresses within a block
